mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits an easing shock to equity valuation by lowering the discount rate on future earnings, so sustained declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during periods when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above a recent trend level signals deteriorating household income expectations and weaker final demand, which depresses near-term S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the sign of the effect flips positive in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening domestic demand across consumption and housing, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive RSAFS and HOUST growth does not outperform single-series signals in explaining 60-day SPX returns, or if positive-return hit rate falls below 55% during periods when both series improve together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to prior months reflects stronger industrial output and a firmer production backdrop, which lowers recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with INDPRO gains above 0.4% are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals weaker aggregate demand and lower forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative on the latest quarterly print
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or if the sign relationship fails in backtests across at least two recessionary windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates stay deeply negative and the liquidity backdrop supports equities, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% m/m and FEDFUNDS < CPI inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are modeled separately.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE reading combined with expanding PAYEMS indicates labor market tightness without immediate recession stress, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.5% and PAYEMS rises month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS condition does not improve 20-day SPX hit rate versus either series alone, or if triggered episodes produce negative returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A strong HOUST rebound after weakness signals housing-led demand recovery and better cyclical expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in post-shock recoveries.
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebound episodes do not deliver positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal is neutral/negative in a rolling 3-year evaluation window.
